career_angle: Don't pivot your career toward Terafab roles yet. If you want semiconductor experience, apply to TSMC, Intel, or Samsung where chips actually get made. Watch for actual equipment orders and ASML partnerships before taking this seriously.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Bloomberg is credible but reporting Musk's own claims uncritically. Source is the subject himself, not independent verification.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Zero evidence presented -- no site plans, no equipment orders, no hiring announcements, no partnerships with ASML or other lithography vendors. Just a statement of intent.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: No independent verification possible. No filings, no permits, no capital allocation disclosed. Entirely based on Musk's verbal claim.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Musk is CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, controls xAI. Announcement benefits stock price and narrative control. Joint venture structure across his own companies raises governance red flags.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Semiconductor industry experts widely skeptical. Building a leading-edge fab from scratch takes $20B+ and 5+ years even for experienced players like Intel. No analyst endorsements cited.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Vague 'advanced technology fab' with 'all equipment necessary to make chips of any kind' is hand-waving. No process node, no capacity targets, no timeline, no capex figure.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Musk's track record on manufacturing timelines is abysmal. Cybertruck delayed 3+ years, Full Self-Driving promised since 2016, Optimus robot demos staged. The article itself notes his 'history of over-promising.'
